source_text: /Users/pranava/Projects/FERC Show Cause Orders/sources/comments/files/20251121-5188__Eversource-Energy-Service-Company/RM26-4 ANOPR Eversource Energy Comments.txt
overall_summary: Eversource supports establishing rules to clarify the large load interconnection process but opposes overly prescriptive requirements. The company advocates for regulatory flexibility to accommodate varying load types and service arrangements, preservation of existing retail transmission arrangements for large loads, and optional participation in generator cluster studies rather than mandatory enrollment.

quotes.text: an overly prescriptive and restrictive final rule arising out of this proceeding could result in more harm than the status quo to prospective large load customers, interconnecting transmission owners, and the public generally
quotes.concern: Overly prescriptive rules risk harming large load interconnection process

quotes.text: Eversource recommends that the focus of any final rule arising out of this proceeding focus on improving the efficiency of the large-load interconnection process without being overly prescriptive
quotes.concern: Prioritize efficiency improvements over rigid standardization

quotes.text: implementing a strict, one-size-fits-all interconnection process will impede rather than facilitate the interconnection of these large loads
quotes.concern: One-size-fits-all approach will obstruct large load interconnection

quotes.text: Given the extreme complexities and variability of the types of loads and their desired service arrangements, the Commission should also ensure that any final rule provides sufficient flexibility to address the different needs of large-load customers
quotes.concern: Final rules must accommodate diverse load types and service needs

quotes.text: Eversource appreciates that the ANOPR appears not to disturb, and should not disturb, certain existing beneficial transmission arrangements between utilities and their retail customers. These arrangements should also remain available to prospective large-load customers as they are often faster and more flexible than traditional federal interconnection processes
quotes.concern: Preserve retail transmission arrangements for large loads

quotes.text: In Eversource's experience, prospective large loads overwhelmingly prefer the retail construct
quotes.concern: Large load customers favor retail service arrangements

quotes.text: the retail arrangement for transmission-interconnected electric service provides significant advantages to the customer as it allows the customer to function as an end-use, state-jurisdictional customer for the sale of electricity while simultaneously allowing the transmission provider to deploy its own expertise in the complex federal interconnection space
quotes.concern: Retail arrangements combine state jurisdiction with transmission expertise

quotes.text: state processes must necessarily account for regional differences and be more responsive to local needs, including potentially the flexibility required by large loads
quotes.concern: State processes better accommodate regional and local needs

quotes.text: Retail processes can in some cases also result in the timelier interconnection of loads relative to federal-jurisdictional process, primarily due to the fixed nature of cluster study processes required by Order No. 2023
quotes.concern: Retail pathways enable faster interconnection than federal cluster studies

quotes.text: the Commission should preserve both existing and prospective retail interconnections at transmission voltages in order to efficiently interconnect large loads to the system
quotes.concern: Preserve retail option for transmission-voltage loads

quotes.text: While combined studies could result in enhanced efficiencies, the strict and often lengthy study timelines for generator interconnection may not be practically feasible with the tight timelines for the interconnection of large loads, which can either be subject to state-mandated service dates (such as through electrification) or whose viability is based on rapid development and construction (such as data centers)
quotes.concern: Generator cluster timelines conflict with load project urgency

quotes.text: to the extent any final rule arising out of this proceeding contemplates including qualifying loads in the Order No. 2023-mandated cluster study process, such inclusion should be optional
quotes.concern: Large load cluster study participation must be optional not mandatory

quotes.text: This will allow loads without major time pressures to reap the benefits and efficiencies of the cluster study process (such as reduced network upgrade costs) while not threatening the viability of other load projects for whom the cluster study process and timeline present insurmountable barriers to entry
quotes.concern: Optional cluster participation balances efficiency gains and project viability

quotes.text: the Commission should consider not including loads in that process at all due to the time pressures described above
quotes.concern: Consider excluding loads entirely from generator cluster study process

quotes.text: In Eversource's experience, none of its current or prospective large load customers have expressed any interest in mandatory/dispatchable curtailment
quotes.concern: Large load customers lack interest in mandatory curtailment requirements

bins.name: Regulatory flexibility for diverse load types
bins.description: Eversource supports final rules that prioritize efficiency and flexibility rather than prescriptive uniformity. Load characteristics vary widely by purpose, use, location, and service needs, and rigid one-size-fits-all approaches will obstruct interconnection. Any rule must accommodate different load types, hybrid arrangements, and regional variations.

bins.name: Preserve retail transmission service pathways
bins.description: Eversource opposes any changes that would eliminate state-jurisdictional retail arrangements for transmission-interconnected loads. Large load customers strongly prefer retail service, which combines state regulatory simplicity with transmission provider expertise, enables faster interconnection than federal cluster processes, and accounts for regional and local needs. Both existing and prospective retail interconnections should remain available.

bins.name: Optional rather than mandatory cluster study participation for loads
bins.description: Eversource opposes mandatory participation of large loads in generator cluster study processes. Generator cluster study timelines are incompatible with the urgent service dates and rapid development cycles of large load projects like electrification and data centers. Participation should remain optional, and the Commission should consider excluding loads from the cluster process entirely to preserve project viability.

bins.name: Limited demand for mandatory curtailment among load customers
bins.description: Eversource reports that none of its current or prospective large load customers have expressed interest in mandatory or dispatchable curtailment arrangements. This finding suggests customer demand may not support regulatory requirements for curtailment functionality.
